author: @JustavvaGo
text: Thanks a lot Robert, i learnt this about 3 weeks ago. Got a small bike wheel and used it as gearing, not enough wind power to turn the rotor blades. Bigger blades needed or, less gearing.

author: @offgridwanabe
text: Gravity towers loaded with an EV which rolled up the hill to load in the tower, so the question is can a power advantage be had by using rolling on wheels to get up a hill to using the weight of the vehicle in a straight gravity to turn the generator  to charge the battery. I think not but it could cut down on electric from the grid.

author: @rfiskillingussoftly6568
text: If you turn contraption on its it's side with the big gear at the top...you could put a universal joint on top of the big gear ..connected a big flywheel with a heavy weighting on a third of the flywheel the taller the better ...another shaft going up from the center of the flywheel for leverage...now when you move the shaft around in a small circle...the heavy weight will fall in a big circle...the off balanced weight we help you turn it"  and those magnetic gears will cap it off!  Another great video man!
